---
title: "Henry's Law Reference Page<br/>*Oh, Henry!* "
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *Oh, Henry!*

Main title explanation

## Oh, Henry!

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/dCOQy1C7yf0?si=TKD1qWmpW3LuX1__" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](73-Visuals.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Henry's Law Example](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/SingleComponentExamples.ods) – A spreadsheet with the sample calculations from the video

## Definitions

[Clapeyron Equation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clausius%E2%80%93Clapeyron_relation#Exact_Clapeyron_equation)
: A thermodynamic equation relating the slope of the vapor-pressure/temperature curve to the enthalpy of vaporization, the absolute temperature, the molar volume of the vapor phase. and the molar volume of the liquid phase. $\frac{dp^*}{dT} = \frac{\Delta \hat{H}_{v}}{T(\hat{V}_{g} - \hat{V}_l)}$

[Clausius-Clapeyron Equation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clausius%E2%80%93Clapeyron_relation)
: A equation relating the vapor pressure of a pure substance to the enthalpy of vaporization and the absolute temperature, assuming that enthalpy of vaporization doesn't vary with temperature. $\ln p^{*} = -\frac{\Delta \hat{H}_v}{RT} + B$

[Dew-Point Temperature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dew_point)
: The temperature at which a condensible vapor will begin to condense from a gas/vapor mixture

[Antoine Equation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antoine_equation)
: An empirical equation relating vapor pressure to temperature for a pure substance. One form is $\log_{10} p^{*} = A - \frac{B}{T + C}$

Relative Saturation or [Relative Humidity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidity#Relative_humidity)
: The ratio of the partial pressure of a condensible vapor to its vapor pressure. $s_r \text{ or }h_r = \frac{p_i}{p_i^*(T)}*100%$

Molal Saturation or Molal Humidity
: The ratio of the moles of vapor to the moles of vapor-free gas. $s_m\text{ or }h_m = \frac{p_i} {P-p_i} = \frac{\text{moles of vapor}}{\text{moles of vapor-free (dry)gas}}$

Absolute Saturation or [Absolute Humidity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humidity#Absolute_humidity)
: $s_a\text{ or }h_a = \frac{p_{i} M_{i}} {(P-p_i)M_{dry}} = \frac{\text{mass of vapor}}{\text{mass of vapor-free (dry)gas}}$

Percentage Saturation or Percentage Humidity
: $s_p\text{ or }h_p = 100\% \times \frac{s_m} {s_m^*} = 100\% \times \frac{\frac{p_i}{P-p_i}}{\frac{p_i^*}{P-p_i^*}}$

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Can You Tell an Azeotrope from a Peritectic? Get Your Phase Diagrams Right! Part 2**<br/>*Ternary Phase Diagrams, Lever Rule*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/QBREikNSvGI), [Info Page](../../balances/material/ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/71-IntroMultiphasePart2Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!**<br/>*Graphical BUBL P, DEW P, BUBL T, DEW T, and FLASH calculations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/shKvpcsJIEM), [Info Page](../../balances/material/GraphicalDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/72-BubbleDewFlashVisuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 1**<br/>*Raoult's law, BUBL P, DEW P*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/vSkSRi6HYVQ), [Info Page](../../balances/material/RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/74-Visuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 2**<br/>*BUBL T, DEW T*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/9GyrfJzg-tg), [Info Page](../../balances/material/RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/75-Visuals.qmd)
